title: antennaria aprica var. aureola
intro: Antennaria aprica var. aureola, also known as golden ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of golden ragwort include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health. Herbal preparations of Antennaria aprica var. aureola typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
